name: Diane Belliveau
enriched_description: Leads Export Development Canada's Oceania business development from Sydney, originating corporate loans and supporting Canadian exporters across energy, infrastructure and mining in Australia and New Zealand. A useful first port of call for Canadian companies evaluating ANZ market entry, project finance or local partnerships.
proof_points: 20+ years in international business development and financial services
Joined EDC in 1999; previously Regional Manager for Mexico and Africa
Board member, Canadian Australian Chamber of Commerce

name: William Johnson
enriched_description: Helps stand up and run Austrade's Southeast Asia Landing Pads in Ho Chi Minh City and Jakarta, supporting Australian tech scaleups in fintech, AI, cyber, quantum and cleantech to expand into the region. Previously advised inbound investors from the Americas, Europe, Middle East and Africa on Australian market entry, FIRB processes and partner introductions.
proof_points: Helped launch Austrade Landing Pads in Jakarta and Ho Chi Minh City
Sector focus: Fintech, AI, Cyber, Quantum, Cleantech

name: Madeline Ferguson
enriched_description: Directs Austrade's global Landing Pads program, which gives Australian tech companies hands-on market entry support, workspace and curated introductions across Singapore, San Francisco, the UK, Ho Chi Minh City and Jakarta. Useful contact for founders mapping international expansion pathways or seeking government-backed soft-landing infrastructure.
proof_points: Oversees 5 Landing Pads markets: Singapore, San Francisco, UK, Ho Chi Minh City, Jakarta
Previously Trade and Investment Commissioner roles in Vietnam, Cambodia and New York

name: David Camerlengo
enriched_description: Heads Investment NSW's Investment and Industry function, leading attraction and acceleration of major projects into New South Wales, including work with the Investment Delivery Authority. Previously Austrade's Senior Trade & Investment Commissioner for the UK and Ireland, with deep experience advising international corporates on Australian market entry across energy, advanced manufacturing and infrastructure.
proof_points: Former Austrade Senior Trade & Investment Commissioner, UK & Ireland
16 years in the USA including President, North America at EGR Group
MSc Technology Commercialization, UT Austin McCombs

name: Carol Friel
enriched_description: Leads precinct development at Investment NSW, building out innovation precincts and hubs (including the Western Sydney Startup Hub) to grow scaleups in NSW priority sectors. Originally from Ireland, Carol is a useful contact for international founders seeking landing-pad style infrastructure, ecosystem introductions and entrepreneurship programs in Sydney.
proof_points: Helped launch the Western Sydney Startup Hub
Previously Director, Arts & Entrepreneurship and Associate Director, Innovation Hubs at Investment NSW
MA Dublin City University; BA NUI Maynooth

name: Emily Cusick
enriched_description: Sits within Investment NSW's Investment Delivery Authority as Senior Associate, Investment Concierge & Strategic Advisory, providing case-managed support to international investors landing major projects in New South Wales. Background spans technology and cyber investment partnerships and private-sector investment management, making her a practical contact for inbound corporates navigating NSW approvals, sites and incentives.
proof_points: Part of NSW's Investment Delivery Authority case management team
Previously Associate, Industry & Investment Partnerships (Tech & Cyber) at Investment NSW

name: David Smith
enriched_description: Runs program operations at Investment NSW, designing and delivering startup and scaleup programs for the NSW innovation ecosystem. Previously created and led startup programs at the University of Sydney's INCUBATE accelerator, and brings founder and angel investor experience to advising early-stage companies on go-to-market, programs and capital pathways in Australia.
proof_points: Creator and Head of Startup Programs at USYD INCUBATE
Active angel investor and multi-time founder

name: Kutay Kesim
enriched_description: Operates dual roles as Senior Export Adviser at Investment NSW and a TradeStart Adviser delivered in partnership with Austrade, providing free, tailored coaching to NSW SMEs on entering and scaling in offshore markets. Useful counterpart for international advisers and chambers coordinating two-way trade with NSW exporters across goods, services and tech.
proof_points: 15+ years in international trade
TradeStart Adviser in partnership with Austrade
Based at Investment NSW, Martin Place Sydney

name: Cindy Callander
enriched_description: Represents Scottish Development International from Melbourne as Senior Trade Specialist for Science and Technology, connecting Scottish companies (fintech, space, life sciences, advanced engineering) with Australian customers, partners and investors. Previously Senior Policy Analyst at Global Victoria, with prior roles at Trade and Investment Queensland, giving her broad cross-jurisdiction experience.
proof_points: 24+ years in trade and investment roles
Previously Senior Policy Analyst at Global Victoria
Earlier roles with Trade and Investment Queensland

name: Brooke Roberts
enriched_description: Sharesies is a NZ-born micro-investing platform that opened share market access from a $5 minimum. As Co-Founder and Director, Roberts brings frontline experience scaling a regulated fintech across NZ and Australia, raising capital, and building a brand around financial inclusion. Useful for founders tackling consumer fintech, trans-Tasman expansion, and product-led growth.
proof_points: Co-founded Sharesies in 2017
Platform reached ~700,000 active users across NZ and Australia
Joint winner, 2020 NZ Women of Influence Award (Business Enterprise)

name: Jason Marsham
enriched_description: WeBridge Partners is a Sydney-based consultancy helping asset management, wealth management and fund admin firms scope and execute platform transformations. As Founder, Marsham draws on a London-New York-Sydney career across asset management trading desks, hedge fund operations and middle-market financial services. Helpful for UK and European fintech and wealth firms entering Australia.

name: Zach Zocher
enriched_description: Techvisa is an Australian migration agency specialising in visa and global mobility solutions for technology companies and talent across Australia, Singapore, the US and the UK. As Head of Community, Zocher works with founders and scale-ups to navigate Australian immigration law, sponsor international hires, and unlock global talent pools. A practical resource for any tech company relocating staff into Australia.

name: Laurie Price
enriched_description: Foley is a Sydney and Brisbane-based international trade and investment advisory that helps companies succeed in cross-border markets. As Principal Consultant, Price advises on market entry, business development and stakeholder engagement, drawing on a UK-to-Australia career across government, university and private-sector roles in the education and training sector. Holds a Master of Development Studies from UNSW.

name: Olwyn Connolly
enriched_description: Squeeze is a SaaS platform for the accounting profession, helping firms and small business owners run smoother operations. As Co-Founder and Group COO, Connolly leads operations, scales teams and brings over a decade of digital and people transformation across the accounting sector. Also Founder at Practice Connections Advisory and a GAICD; a strong resource for Irish and European SaaS founders moving into Australia.
proof_points: GAICD (Australian Institute of Company Directors)
Masters in Accounting, University of Galway

name: Brian Walsh
enriched_description: East West World Private Equity is a Sydney-based firm focused on capital formation for the animation, digital media and creative industries, structuring co-venture alliances across production, technology and finance. Founder Brian Walsh brings a US-to-Australia career spanning venture capital, growth-stage capital raising and creative-industry deal-making. Useful for founders in animation, gaming, IP-led media and other creative sectors.

name: Brendan O'Connell
enriched_description: AgriTech New Zealand is the membership-funded industry body for NZ's agritech sector, launched in 2018 to grow the country's agricultural technology ecosystem. As Chief Executive, O'Connell leads sector strategy, international missions and member growth, drawing on 16+ years in the primary sector and a prior role as NZ Pathfinder at Enterprise Ireland. A strong contact for Irish and European agritech founders eyeing ANZ.
proof_points: Chief Executive of AgriTech New Zealand
Former NZ Pathfinder at Enterprise Ireland
16+ years in the primary sector

name: Daniel Grindrod
enriched_description: LaunchPad is positioned as APAC's leading GTM community, helping tech sales professionals advance their careers and SaaS companies build strong go-to-market teams. Co-Founder Daniel Grindrod brings a UK-to-Sydney path across talent acquisition and SaaS scaling, and also runs 4Twenty Consulting and Tech Talks Australia. Useful for UK and European SaaS founders building APAC GTM teams from Sydney.
proof_points: Co-Founder of LaunchPad
Co-Founder & Director of 4Twenty Consulting (since 2016)
Founder of Tech Talks Australia

name: Jerome Fournand
enriched_description: FACCI, the French-Australian Chamber of Commerce & Industry, is the bilateral business network connecting French and Australian companies through events, advocacy and trade services. As General Manager since 2019, Fournand leads operations across FACCI's chapters and coordinates with Team France (Embassy, Consulate, Alliance Francaise). Brings a French-to-Australia career spanning Microsoft France, startups and Sydney operations leadership.
proof_points: General Manager of FACCI since March 2019
Former Head of Operations, GDR Group (Sydney)
Began career at Microsoft France
